duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Bot-initiated yield liquidation uses yieldAccounts[msg.sender] instead of sd.yieldAccount, causing _unstake/_repay to resolve wrong yield account when botAdmin calls, rendering the entire forced-liquidation mechanism non-functional
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the identical code defect: yieldAccounts[msg.sender] in _unstake/_repay resolves to botAdmin's account instead of the NFT owner's sd.yieldAccount. TECH-H001 has the most precise line references (235-270), comprehensive technical analysis, and covers all affected downstream functions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Yield cap check in executeYieldBorrowERC20 uses groupData.borrowIndex instead of assetData.supplyIndex to compute totalSupply, inflating the cap denominator and allowing over-borrowing
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 12 threats describe the same calculation error: VaultLogic.erc20GetTotalCrossSupply(assetData, groupData.borrowIndex) should use assetData.supplyIndex. TECH-H012 is rated CRITICAL with the most detailed investigation rationale and precise line references. Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the instance titled 'Yield cap bypass: yield borrow uses borrowIndex instead of supplyIndex'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Bot-initiated repay blocked when claimed yield < debt+fine because safeTransferFrom(nftOwner) requires user's WETH approval, which user can revoke to permanently prevent liquidation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the same DoS vector: underlyingAsset.safeTransferFrom(vars.nftOwner,...) in _repay requires nftOwner cooperation during bot-driven liquidation. TECH-H003 provides the most specific code evidence (lines 329-331) and clearly distinguishes this from the yield account resolution bug. Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the instance titled 'Malicious User Can Prevent Liquidation by Revoking WETH Approval'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unstake fines (WETH/DAI) collected during _repay permanently locked in YieldStaking contracts: totalUnstakeFine only incremented never decremented, no ERC20 sweep function exists
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 threats describe the same fund-locking defect: totalUnstakeFine only increases in _unstake, never decreases in _repay, and no sweep mechanism exists for ERC20 tokens. TECH-H004 provides the most precise line references (263-264) and clearest technical description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PriceOracle.getAssetPriceFromChainlink missing time-based staleness check (block.timestamp - updatedAt < MAX_STALENESS), allowing indefinite use of stale prices during Chainlink feed outages
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the identical missing validation: no require(block.timestamp - updatedAt < MAX_STALENESS) after latestRoundData(). TECH-H005 has the most precise line references (81-89) and clearest technical pattern description. Note: both instances of TECH2-H007 are removed (one titled 'Missing Staleness Check', one titled 'Dust-debt rounding' which belongs to DUP-008).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PoolManager.dispatch() can delegatecall to address(0) if module uninstalled but proxy remains trusted, causing silent no-op with empty return data
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 4 threats describe the same missing check: no require(moduleImpl != address(0)) before delegatecall in dispatch(). TECH-H006 has the most specific line references (43-57) and clearest severity justification.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: YieldSavingsDai.getAccountTotalUnstakedYield arithmetic underflow when sDAI balance drops below recorded accountYieldInWithdraws, blocking all new stakes for affected yield accounts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the same underflow: balance - inWithdraw reverts in Solidity 0.8+ when sDAI value drops. TECH-H007 provides the most detailed technical analysis including the full call chain through _stake → _convertToYieldSharesWithTotalYield. Note: TECH2-H008 here refers to the instance titled 'Underflow in YieldSavingsDai Total Unstaked Yield Blocks Staking'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Integer division truncation in GenericLogic._getUserERC20DebtInBaseCurrency rounds small debt amounts to zero, allowing invisible debt accumulation across groups that evades health factor checks
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats describe the same truncation: (assetPrice * userTotalDebt) / (10 ** decimals) rounds to 0 for small debts. TECH-H008 provides the most specific line references (200-207) and clearest explanation. Note: TECH2-H004 here is the 'Dust Debt Rounding' instance; TECH2-H007 here is the 'Dust-debt rounding to zero' instance.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Debt share desynchronization when external caller directly calls yieldRepayERC20 bypassing YieldStakingBase, reducing pool debt without updating totalDebtShare
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same vulnerability: direct yieldRepayERC20 calls reduce getTotalDebt() without updating totalDebtShare, deflating the debt-per-share ratio. TECH-H009 has more comprehensive analysis covering the full impact chain. Note: TECH2-H006 here is the 'Debt Share Manipulation via Direct Repayment' instance.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee collection in executeCollectFeeToTreasury reads and zeroes accruedFee before calling updateInterestIndexs, creating potential accounting inconsistency with post-update normalizedIncome
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same sequencing issue: accruedFee zeroed before updateInterestIndexs, then amountToCollect computed with post-update normalizedIncome. OPS-H015 provides the most thorough analysis of both accounting and cap-check implications.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: YieldStakingBase.createYieldAccount is publicly callable for any user address without msg.sender == user check, allowing front-running and griefing
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same missing access control on YieldStakingBase.createYieldAccount(address user). TECH-H011 has more specific line references (118-124) and analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: YieldRegistry.createYieldAccount lacks pause/nonReentrant guards and access restrictions, allowing mass clone-spam griefing by any caller for whitelisted managers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe missing guards on YieldRegistry.createYieldAccount. TECH2-H008 (the 'YieldRegistry.createYieldAccount is callable by anyone' instance) provides broader impact analysis. Note: TECH2-H008 here refers to the instance titled 'YieldRegistry.createYieldAccount is callable by anyone and can be griefed'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Yield group added to asset's groupList via executeSetAssetYieldEnable without setting rateModel, causing updateInterestRates to revert when calling IInterestRateModel(address(0)), bricking all asset operations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same configuration ordering vulnerability: yield group created without rateModel, causing address(0) call in updateInterestRates. ECON2-H008 is rated CRITICAL with the most comprehensive impact analysis and specific deployment evidence from InitConfigYield.s.sol.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Yield.yieldBorrowERC20 accessible by any address with non-zero yieldCap, with no explicit collateral/allowlist check beyond cap gating, enabling uncollateralized borrowing if cap misconfigured
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same access control gap: yieldBorrowERC20 enforces only yield caps, not collateral or allowlist. ECON-H008 provides more detailed attack economics and clearer precondition analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unbounded iteration over EnumerableSet groupList/assetList/suppliedAssets/borrowedAssets in core operations can exceed gas limits as protocol scales, causing DoS on critical paths
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same gas DoS via unbounded loops over dynamic sets. TECH-H013 lists more affected functions and has broader scope analysis. Note: TECH2-H005 here is the 'Unbounded iteration over asset/group sets' instance.
